title: ForgeCandidateScore
description: Structured output returned by one agent evaluating ONE candidate option in comparative / judge-panel mode (choosing among N alternatives — beachheads, wedges, entry angles, domains, monetization models). Use instead of ForgeBranchVerdict when the question is 'which option wins', not 'does this claim hold'. One object per candidate; all candidates in a round are scored on the SAME shared criteria so scores are comparable. A cross-cutting 'decider' agent may reuse this schema with `candidate` set to the deciding dimension and `criteria` listing the options ranked on it.

candidate.description: The option this agent evaluated, restated in one sharp sentence.

score.description: Overall strength as THE option to pursue, 0-100. Must be comparable across agents in the same round — anchor it to the shared criteria, not to a private scale.

criteria.description: Rating on each of the round's shared criteria — the SAME criteria for every candidate (e.g. budget-now, reachability, moat/neutrality, fit, timing).

rating.description: How this candidate rates on the criterion.

rank_rationale.description: Why this candidate should rank where it does relative to the alternatives — the comparative claim, not just an absolute description.

recommendation.description: PURSUE = the best entry / the wedge. RUNNER_UP = viable but not first. DROP = not worth entering via this option.

claim.description: The single most decisive concrete finding for or against this candidate — a named competitor, funding round, budget/market number, regulation with date, or benchmark.

source.description: Citation: URL, paper, or named source. Required for every non-obvious claim.

confidence.description: Established = multi-source. Reported = single/secondary. Vendor/Projection = marketing/forecast. Contested = sources disagree. Open = unresolved.

opportunity_signals.description: Openings specific to this candidate — unmet demand, incumbent holes, timing tailwinds. May be empty.
